concerns.text: T013a (Shapiro-Wilk/Log-transform) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on T013 (metrics.csv creation) and produces a new artifact (metrics_transformed.csv) that T014a/T015 must consume. The task description implies a sequential flow (T013 -> T013a -> T014a), yet the [P] tag suggests it can run in parallel with T013 or T014a, violating the 'Producer before consumer' rule. T013a must be sequential.

T014a (Physical Descriptors) and T015 (Thermal Conductivity Extraction) are both marked as NOT parallel due to dependency on T013, but the text implies they are independent of each other. However, T016 (Correlation) depends on BOTH T014a and T015. The current ordering allows T014a and T015 to run in parallel (which is correct), but the description for T014a says 'MUST run AFTER T013' and T015 says 'MUST run AFTER T013'. The dependency chain is clear, but the [P] removal logic is inconsistent with the parallel opportunity between T014a and T015. T014a and T015 should be marked [P] relative to each other, but not relative to T013.

T020a (VIF Calculation) is marked [P] in the list but the description explicitly states 'T020a-T024 are strictly sequential and CANNOT be run in parallel'. Furthermore, T020a depends on T014a (Physical Descriptors) which is in US2. The task list places T020a in US3 but marks it [P], which is a direct contradiction. T020a must be unmarked [P] and placed strictly after T014a completion.
T021 (VIF Filtering) is marked [P] but the description states 'T021 MUST run after T020a' and 'T020a-T024 are strictly sequential'. The [P] tag is incorrect and misleading. T021 must be unmarked [P].

T022 (Model Training) is marked [P] but depends on T021. T023 (Cross-Validation) depends on T022. T024 (Aggregation) depends on T023. The entire chain T020a->T024 is sequential. Marking any of these as [P] violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule. All must be unmarked [P].

T018a, T018b, T018c are marked as NOT parallel, which is correct, but they are listed as separate tasks in a way that suggests they could be parallel if not for the note. The dependency chain T018a -> T018b -> T018c is explicit. However, T016 (Correlation) depends on T015, and T017 (Bonferroni) depends on T016. The ordering of T016, T017, T018a/b/c is ambiguous. T018a/b/c (Power Analysis) logically depends on the final sample size 'n' which is determined after T015 (skipping materials). T016 and T017 also depend on T015. The current list does not clearly enforce that T018a/b/c run after T016/T017 or if they are independent. Given T018b calculates alpha based on 'n', and T016/T017 use alpha, the order T015 -> T016 -> T017 -> T018a -> T018b -> T018c seems implied but not explicitly enforced in the dependency list for T016/T017.

T013a is marked [P] but the description says 'If non-normal, apply log-transformation...'. This is a conditional transformation that depends on the output of T013. It cannot run in parallel with T013. The [P] tag is a semantic error.

T020c is marked [P] but it is a 'Define input/output schema' task. While schema definition is often independent, in this context it is part of the 'VIF calculation' block (T020a-T020d) which is described as a strict sequential chain. The [P] tag is inconsistent with the 'strictly sequential' note for the group.
